title: Boat Rental in Hardangerfjord: Tips, Costs and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Hardangerfjord, Norway's second-longest fjord, offers a stunning blend of steep mountains, waterfalls and fruit orchards. Renting a boat here typically costs from EUR 250 for a small motorboat half-day to EUR 1,500 for a crewed yacht, and platforms like BoatHire24 make booking straightforward. A great departure point is the town of Norheimsund, where several operators offer daily hire.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Hardangerfjord?

Prices range from EUR 80 for a shared tour to EUR 1,800 for a luxury crewed yacht per day. Budget-conscious visitors can join a group sightseeing trip from Eidfjord harbour for around EUR 80 per person, while a private half-day hire of a 6-metre motorboat from Utne or Lofthus starts at EUR 250. For greater comfort, a 12-metre catamaran sleeping up to ten guests costs about EUR 900 for a full day, and a crewed Azimut 50 flybridge from Rosendal marina nears EUR 1,500. Compare verified options and see the latest prices via the yacht charter Hardangerfjord page.

  • Shared sightseeing tour from Eidfjord: EUR 80 per person, 2 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours) from Norheimsund: EUR 250 for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours) from Utne: EUR 900 for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day from Rosendal: EUR 1,500, 1,800 for up to 10 guests.

Where to set off in Hardangerfjord?

Popular departure points include Norheimsund, Eidfjord, Utne, Lofthus, Rosendal and Jondal. Norheimsund offers a well-sheltered marina with easy access to the main fjord and the Steinsdalsfossen waterfall. Eidfjord sits at the innermost end, close to the Vøringsfossen waterfall and Hardangervidda National Park. Utne provides a quieter base on the southern shore, while Lofthus is famous for its cherry orchards and offers direct access to deep-water anchorages. Rosendal, near the Baroniet Rosendal manor, has a modern harbour with fuel and supplies, and Jondal is ideal for those wanting to explore the Folgefonna glacier area.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Small motorboat, best for fishing and exploring narrow inlets, up to 6 guests, from EUR 250 half-day.
  2. Bowrider or day cruiser, ideal for a family day trip to Bondhusvatnet, up to 8 guests, from EUR 400 half-day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42), spacious and stable for a group of friends, up to 12 guests, from EUR 900 full-day.
  4. Flybridge motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), luxury with sun deck, suitable for longer cruises to Rosendal, up to 10 guests, from EUR 1,400 full-day.
  5. Fairline Targa 43, sporty yet comfortable, perfect for a day exploring the Sørfjord arm, up to 8 guests, from EUR 1,000 full-day; see the Fairline listing for exact availability and pricing.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Hardangerfjord?

No international licence is required for boats under 8 metres and 25 hp, but operators set their own rules. Many boat rental companies in Hardangerfjord allow rental without a licence for smaller motorboats after a safety briefing. For larger yachts above 30 hp or 8 metres, you may need to provide a boating licence or proof of experience, such as an ICC or equivalent. The operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking.

If you plan to explore the Sørfjord arm all the way to Odda, leave by 09:00 to avoid afternoon winds that can create choppy conditions near Bondhusbreen glacier.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80, 1,800 per person/day
  • Peak months: June, July, August 2026
  • Trip length: 2 hours (shared) to 8 hours (private)
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on most boats
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Hardangerfjord

Can I rent a boat in Hardangerfjord without a licence?

Yes, many small motorboats up to 25 hp can be rented after a short briefing, but confirm with the operator when booking.

How long does it take to cruise from Norheimsund to Eidfjord?

The distance is roughly 45 nautical miles, and at 20 knots the journey takes about 2 hours 15 minutes one way.

What is the best time of day for calm waters?

Mornings until 11:00 are generally calmest; afternoon breezes, especially in July, can create short, choppy waves.

Are there any guided tours for solo travellers?

Yes, several operators in Eidfjord and Utne offer shared tours from EUR 80 per person, visiting waterfalls and fruit farms.

Whether you are planning a quiet day exploring waterfalls or a week-long fjord adventure, BoatHire24 connects you with trusted boat owners across Hardangerfjord. Owners can also list their own vessel on the platform to reach a worldwide audience. BoatHire24 makes it easy to hire a boat in over 100 destinations.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Hardangerfjord and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
